title: Test Layers, Service Containers, Coverage, and Changed-Case Evidence · Release Evidence Brief: Ci Test Report Quarantine Policy
summary: A green unit suite mocks every boundary, integration tests share mutable state, service containers have no readiness check, and coverage increases while production behavior regresses. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci test report quarantine policy increment in layered test and service evidence plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Retain machine-readable reports, failed-case diagnostics, traces, screenshots, retries, quarantine owner, expiry, and re-entry criteria.
Interpret statement, branch, function, changed-line, mutation, and risk coverage as evidence with explicit exclusions and blind spots.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example suite mocks every boundary, starts an unready database, shares state across 4 shards, retries flakes, and celebrates coverage while a changed case fails. Design risk-layered, isolated, diagnostic evidence. Competency probe: Retain machine-readable reports, failed-case diagnostics, traces, screenshots, retries, quarantine owner, expiry, and re-entry criteria. A defensible solution applies Ci Test Report Quarantine Policy, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to layered test and service evidence plan.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-worked-ci-testing-services-coverage-4
  - competency: ci-worked-ci-testing-services-coverage-4
    artifact: test-evidence.yaml
    decision: worked ci testing services coverage 4
    environment: GitHub-hosted ubuntu-24.04 runner on a cold cache
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      layers: [unit, component, integration, contract, e2e, accessibility, security]
      service-image: postgres@sha256:verified
      service-health: readiness-query
      state: per-job-fixture
      shard-proof: complete-and-disjoint
      coverage: branch-changed-line-and-mutation
      retry: disabled-by-default
      reports: immutable-junit-trace-screenshot
    verify: run changed-case tests with fresh service state deterministic seeds report reconciliation and failure preservation
    failure: Reject coverage percentage as assertion quality, start as readiness, shared mutable fixtures, or retry-until-green.
    repair: Select layers by risk, wait for service behavior, isolate state, prove shards, preserve reports, and repair flakes causally.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Retrying until green is an acceptable repair for a flaky required test. This fails the Ci Test Report Quarantine Policy contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
